cases.title: Hahn v. Philadelphia Metro West Presbytery (Cases 2011-11, 2011-12, 2011-15, 2011-16)
cases.synopsis: Steven Hahn filed multiple complaints against Philadelphia Metro West Presbytery (PMWP) arising from his exclusion from worship at Christ the King Presbyterian Church pending a psychiatric evaluation, his subsequent conviction of three charges by PMWP, and PMWP's refusal to institute process against those he accused. The SJC denied all three complaints (2011-11, 2011-12, 2011-15), finding no clear error in PMWP's exercise of deference to the lower court, guilty verdict, or declination to appoint a prosecutor under BCO 31-8.

cases.title: Reese and Bech v. Philadelphia Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: RE Dudley Reese and TE Niel Bech

cases.synopsis: RE Reese and TE Bech complained that Philadelphia Presbytery erred in declaring their complaint out of order after Presbytery refused to allow any questions of TE Jason Hsu regarding his views on women deacons when approving his transfer to City Line Church. The SJC sustained the complaint, finding that deeming parts of a complaint objectionable is insufficient grounds to rule the whole complaint out of order, though it agreed the proposed amends (removing TE Hsu) were not appropriate.
